payload.summary: All four amendments identify genuine, verified defects in the token usage extraction and logging code. Each fix is small, well-scoped, and improves correctness or observability without risk of regression. I confirmed every claim against the actual source at the referenced line numbers.

votes.rationale: Verified at lines 1299-1310: `.as_u64().map(|v| v as u32)` is used for all three token fields. This silently truncates any value above u32::MAX to a wrong number. The Claude path uses serde's native `Option<u32>` deserialization (confirmed at line 1255), which rejects overflow entirely. `u32::try_from(v).ok()` returns None on overflow, which is consistent and correct. Trivial fix, no downside.

Verified at line 186 (Claude path: `Some(p + c)` where p,c are u32) and line 1312 (Codex path: `Some(i + o)` where i,o are u32). Both are unchecked u32 additions that panic in debug builds and wrap silently in release builds. While token counts exceeding u32::MAX is unlikely today, the fix is a single-character change (`checked_add`) that eliminates a real panic path and a silent data corruption path. No reason not to fix this.

Verified at lines 1296-1321: `value.get("usage")` on `{"usage":null}` returns `Some(Value::Null)`, which passes the `let Some(usage)` guard. All subsequent `.get("input_tokens")` etc. on a Null value return None, so the function returns an all-None TokenCounts and stops the reverse scan. A valid earlier `turn.completed` event with real token counts is never reached. This is a confirmed data-loss bug. The fix (skip lines where usage is not an object with at least one recognized field) is correct and the requested regression test will prevent recurrence.

Verified at lines 240-277: `extract_raw_output` (240), `persist_raw_output` (241), `extract_structured_payload` (248), and contract validation (252-256) all use `?` and can return early before the tracing::info! at line 263. Failed or malformed invocations — precisely the ones where token/cost data is most needed for debugging — produce no trace event. Moving or duplicating the log to fire before line 240 (after metadata finalization at line 238) is the correct fix. The metadata including token_counts is already finalized by that point.
